pain_points: No framework for retrieval experimentation and optimization - 3-week engineering project to evaluate new embedding models
Limited context understanding across multi-turn conversations - each query to Elasticsearch is independent and stateless
No ability to combine multiple specialized retrieval strategies - engineers spend 2-3 days writing new Query DSL for new requirements
High maintenance burden for query template management - 40+ Query DSL templates requiring 1-2 weeks to update
Difficulty debugging poor retrieval results - 30-60 minutes per incident with limited visibility
Static query patterns unable to handle complex retrieval needs - 42% of AI Assistant queries require 2+ clarifying questions
Inability to reason about retrieval quality and iterate - 31% of responses rated unhelpful, 68% due to poor document retrieval
use_cases: Intelligent Tool Composition for Complex Queries - automatically compose multiple specialized retrieval tools for complex queries requiring different sources
Self-Optimizing Retrieval with A/B Testing Framework - enable rapid experimentation with different retrieval strategies and automatically measure quality
Adaptive Multi-Step Retrieval with Quality Assessment - replace static single-query retrieval with intelligent agents that evaluate result quality and iterate
Explainable Retrieval for Trust and Debugging - provide complete transparency into retrieval decisions for debugging and trust
Conversation-Aware Contextual Retrieval - maintain retrieval context across multi-turn conversations and refine searches based on previous results
customer_context.scale: 850+ enterprise customer accounts, 45M+ brand assets, 2.5M+ AI assistant conversations monthly, 500K+ automated asset tagging operations daily, 125,000+ daily active users
metrics: 23% of queries return <3 documents indicating poor recall
P95 retrieval latency: 340ms, average 4.2 documents per query
Target: Reduce unhelpful ratings from 31% to <12%, reduce follow-ups from 42% to <20%
31% of responses rated unhelpful, 42% require 2+ follow-up queries
